attributes.description: Allow EC2 to read only the S3 website asset bucket.
attributes.name: cloud-team-playbook-dev-site-assets-read

attributes.user_data:
#!/bin/bash
# Stop on errors, show commands, and fail if a piped command fails.
set -euxo pipefail

# Save all boot messages to a log file and the EC2 console log.
exec > >(tee /var/log/user-data-command-host.log | logger -t user-data -s 2>/dev/console) 2>&1

# Update the instance packages.
dnf update -y

# Install base admin tools.
dnf install -y python3 python3-pip git unzip tar wget jq awscli dnf-plugins-core

# Add the official HashiCorp Amazon Linux repo, then install Terraform.
dnf config-manager --add-repo https://rpm.releases.hashicorp.com/AmazonLinux/hashicorp.repo
dnf install -y terraform

# Install Ansible in a Python virtual environment to avoid changing system Python packages.
python3 -m venv /opt/ansible-venv
/opt/ansible-venv/bin/python -m pip install --upgrade pip
/opt/ansible-venv/bin/python -m pip install --upgrade ansible
ln -sf /opt/ansible-venv/bin/ansible /usr/local/bin/ansible
ln -sf /opt/ansible-venv/bin/ansible-playbook /usr/local/bin/ansible-playbook

# Create a working folder for cloud team scripts.
mkdir -p /opt/cloud-team
cat > /opt/cloud-team/README.txt <<'README'
This is the private command host.
Access it through AWS Systems Manager Session Manager.
Useful checks:
  terraform version
  ansible --version
  aws sts get-caller-identity
  cat /var/log/user-data-command-host.log
README

#!/bin/bash
# Stop on errors, show commands, and fail if a piped command fails.
set -euxo pipefail

# Save all boot messages to a log file and the EC2 console log.
exec > >(tee /var/log/user-data-app.log | logger -t user-data -s 2>/dev/console) 2>&1

# Update the instance packages.
dnf update -y

# Install Apache HTTP server and AWS CLI.
dnf install -y httpd awscli

# Start Apache now and after every reboot.
systemctl enable --now httpd

# Create the web root directory.
mkdir -p /var/www/html

# Copy the website files from the private S3 asset bucket to the local web root.
aws s3 sync "s3://cloud-team-playbook-dev-site-assets-406207085797-cd9d7a2f/" /var/www/html/ --region "us-east-1"

# Set safe ownership for Apache.
chown -R apache:apache /var/www/html

# Restart Apache so it serves the latest files.
systemctl restart httpd

# Write a simple local health file.
echo "ok" > /var/www/html/health.txt

attributes.description: No inbound rules; access is through SSM Session Manager only
attributes.name: cloud-team-playbook-dev-command-sg

attributes.description: Trusted users to ALB app listener on port 7777
ALB to private web EC2 on port 80
